The Joint Maritime Information Center (JMIC) has issued a new advisory stating that the U.S. established southern transit corridor through Omani waters remains open and available to all commercial traffic, despite Iran's declaration yesterday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed.

quoted.text:
the guy who runs the world's largest sovereign wealth fund, $2 trillion, says the hardest part of the job is doing nothing.

Nicolai Tangen has a rule he calls the "inertia analysis":

take your portfolio from january 1st. calculate what it would've returned if you never touched it again. then compare it to what you actually did all year.

"so often you actually have detracted from your performance by the things you've done."

every system you build is fighting the same urge. often the edge isn't the next trade. it's the trades you talk yourself out of.
quoted.author: GoshawkTrades

text: Brent is up 3% on the back of the latest skirmishing on the Gulf, now trading at $79 (still low by wartime standards). #OOTT

Iran gave diplomacy a chance.    

It didn’t work

Now Iran is using force,  with the certainty that talking with the US doesn’t work.

quoted.text:
BREAKING: A source close to Iran's Ghalibaf says in the past two hours Iran's IRGC "has fired at several US vessels attempting to approach Iranian shores," along with "two rogue" commercial vessels.

The source says "the Strait of Hormuz is now completely closed off from all routes and will not reopen in the medium term," adding that "attacks will expand and the conflict will deepen."
